english: Hanoi Grilled Pork with Rice Vermicelli
Hanoi-Style Grilled Pork Vermicelli (Bún Chả Hà Nội)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Bún chả Hà Nội; mapped to broader 'pork dish' class as the closest defensible FoodOn anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not contain a specific code for Bún chả Hà Nội; A0FJP (pork meat dish) is the closest defensible broader category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this Vietnamese composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Bún chả Hà Nội is approximately 400 g, comprising a bowl of rice vermicelli noodles, grilled pork belly slices and pork patties, a bowl of sweetened fish sauce dipping broth, and a plate of fresh herbs and pickled vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Bun Cha (Grilled Pork with Vermicelli) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 for all per-100g nutrient fields. Values are accepted as reported. Note: carbohydrate_g is recorded as 0.0 g/100g in the HPB source for this entry, which is anomalous for a composite dish containing rice vermicelli; this is a known data limitation of the HPB record and is documented in notes. Saturated fat is not reported in the HPB record and is set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bún chả Hà Nội as a composite dish. The GI of 58 is an analogous estimate based on rice vermicelli (GI ~58–61, University of Sydney GI Database, rice noodles entry). The HPB source records carbohydrate as 0 g/100g for this entry, which is a known data anomaly; the dish contains rice vermicelli as a primary component and the glycemic profile is dominated by that component. Glycemic load per serving is calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100; because the HPB-reported carbohydrate for this record is 0 g/100g (anomalous), GL is recorded as 0.0 with a caveat that the true GL for a 400 g serving is estimated at approximately 13–16 based on the vermicelli component alone (approx. 60 g dry vermicelli ≈ 45 g available carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g high-concern threshold; fish sauce in marinade and dipping broth is the primary contributor
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Bún chả Hà Nội due to its very high sodium content of 1050 mg/100g; if consumed, request reduced fish sauce in both the pork marinade and dipping broth, minimise dipping broth intake, and avoid additional pickled vegetables to reduce total sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Rice vermicelli is a high-GI refined carbohydrate (GI ~58–61); dipping broth contains added sugar; the HPB-recorded carbohydrate of 0 g/100g is a known data anomaly and does not reflect the true glycaemic impact of the dish
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should significantly limit portion size of the rice vermicelli component, request reduced sugar in the dipping broth, increase the proportion of fresh herbs and bean sprouts to add fibre, and monitor postprandial blood glucose given the high-GI vermicelli base.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1050 mg/100g) elevates blood pressure risk; pork belly contributes saturated fat and cholesterol (89 mg/100g); combination is unfavourable for cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Bún chả Hà Nội due to its high sodium content and cholesterol load from pork belly; if consumed, choose leaner pork cuts, request reduced fish sauce, limit dipping broth consumption, and keep portion size small.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium worsens tissue swelling; pork belly is rich in saturated fat and arachidonic acid (omega-6) which amplifies inflammatory cytokine production
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute pork belly with leaner pork or chicken, reduce fish sauce in both marinade and dipping broth to lower sodium burden, and monitor individual tolerance to chili (nightshade) which may trigger RA flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bún chả Hà Nội (Hanoi-Style Grilled Pork Vermicelli) is a quintessential Vietnamese dish originating from Hanoi, northern Vietnam. It consists of three components served together: (1) a bowl of thin rice vermicelli noodles (bún), (2) grilled pork — typically both sliced pork belly and small pork patties marinated in fish sauce, garlic, shallots, sugar, and black pepper, chargrilled over charcoal until caramelised — and (3) a warm, slightly sweet and sour dipping broth (nước chấm pha) made from diluted fish sauce, sugar, vinegar or lime juice, garlic, and chili, often containing pickled green papaya and carrots. The dish is served with a generous plate of fresh herbs including mint, perilla, and lettuce. The flavour profile is smoky, savoury, and lightly sweet, with the dipping broth providing a tangy counterpoint to the rich grilled pork. Clinically, the dish is high in sodium (1050 mg/100g per HPB data), primarily from fish sauce in both the marinade and broth, making it a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The pork belly component contributes significant fat and cholesterol (89 mg/100g). The rice vermicelli base has a medium-to-high glycaemic index (~58–61), making portion control important for individuals with type 2 diabetes. The HPB-recorded carbohydrate of 0 g/100g is a known data anomaly for this composite dish entry and does not reflect the true carbohydrate contribution of the vermicelli component. Key nutrients per 100g: energy 171 kcal, protein 19.1 g, fat 10.5 g, sodium 1050 mg, cholesterol 89 mg, iron 1.14 mg.

Bun cha vs bun thit nuong

confidence_reason: All per-100g nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. The carbohydrate anomaly (0 g/100g) is a known limitation of the HPB composite dish entry and is documented; it does not reduce source confidence. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using rice vermicelli as the proxy food.
notes: carbohydrate_g is recorded as 0.0 g/100g and sugar_g as 0.0 g/100g per the HPB source for this composite dish entry. This is a known data anomaly: Bún chả Hà Nội contains rice vermicelli as a primary component and the true carbohydrate content of a full serving is substantially higher. The HPB record likely captures only the grilled pork component without the vermicelli. Users and clinicians should be aware that the glycaemic impact of this dish is dominated by the rice vermicelli component (GI ~58–61). dietary_fibre_g is recorded as 0.0 g/100g per HPB; this is consistent with the pork-only component interpretation. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB source for this specific record entry (pork component only); sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB source for this specific record entry. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this dish. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this dish as of 2026. Glycemic load per serving is recorded as 0.0 due to the HPB carbohydrate anomaly; estimated true GL for a 400 g serving is approximately 13–16 based on the vermicelli component (see gi_source notes).
